GC.observation: CME FedWatch showed the visible 30 Apr 2026 meeting row overwhelmingly concentrated in no change, with ease and hike effectively near zero; classified as a neutral hold.
FRED DFII10 printed 1.90 on 2026-04-17 versus 1.93 on 2026-04-16, a move of less than 5 bps; classified as flat.
TradingView DXY was 98.053, down 0.18% on 2026-04-20; classified as weak/falling.
Cboe VIX was 18.87 on 2026-04-20, which remains in the methodology's balanced 15-20 band.
Atlanta Fed GDPNow remained at 1.3% for 2026:Q1 as of the 2026-04-09 update; classified as slowing.
Hormuz shipping remained constrained and Middle Eastern supply losses were still material; classified as tightening/disruption.
World Gold Council research dated 2026-04-08 said March 2026 saw hefty gold ETF outflows of US$12bn; classified as outflows.

With the Fed on a neutral hold and the ECB also on hold at 2.00%, the EUR-USD policy-rate differential is treated as stable.
TradingView DXY was 98.053, down 0.18% on 2026-04-20; classified as weak/falling.
Cboe VIX was 18.87 on 2026-04-20, which remains in the methodology's balanced 15-20 band.
The ECB's March 2026 staff projections lowered euro-area 2026 growth to 0.9%; classified as slowing.

6A.observation: CME FedWatch showed the visible 30 Apr 2026 meeting row overwhelmingly concentrated in no change, with ease and hike effectively near zero; classified as a neutral hold.
The official RBA statement dated 2026-03-17 raised the cash rate target by 25 bps to 4.10%; classified as hawkish/tightening.
The RBA's 4.10% post-hike cash rate against a neutral Fed leaves the AUD-USD rate differential supportive for AUD; classified as widening in AUD's favor.
TradingView DXY was 98.053, down 0.18% on 2026-04-20; classified as weak/falling.
Broader risk conditions are balanced rather than decisively risk-on or risk-off because VIX stayed below 20 despite rising on the day.
Official Chinese March manufacturing PMI rose to 50.4 from 49.0, returning to expansion; classified as accelerating/improving.
TradingView copper HG1! was 6.0455 USD/lb, down 0.01% on 2026-04-20; classified as falling.

6J.observation: CME FedWatch showed the visible 30 Apr 2026 meeting row overwhelmingly concentrated in no change, with ease and hike effectively near zero; classified as a neutral hold.
The official BoJ statement dated 2026-03-19 kept the overnight call rate around 0.75% in an ongoing normalization setting; classified as hawkish/normalizing.
BoJ normalization versus a neutral Fed narrows the JPY-USD policy gap in the yen's favor; classified as widening in JPY's favor.
TradingView DXY was 98.053, down 0.18% on 2026-04-20; classified as weak/falling.
Cboe VIX was 18.87 on 2026-04-20, which remains in the methodology's balanced 15-20 band.

key_drivers: The Fed remains in a neutral-hold regime while DXY softened on the day, preventing the dollar from overwhelming cross-asset signals.
Crude carries the strongest macro setup because the latest EIA reading showed a draw while Hormuz disruption risk remains elevated.
Equity indices improved after narrowing credit spreads and a softer dollar, but a rising VIX and rising MOVE limited conviction in ES and NQ.
FX divergence remains active: the ECB is on hold, the RBA stays post-hike hawkish, and the BoJ remains in normalization mode while the USD is softer.
Precious metals are mixed because gold ETF flows were negative in March and copper softened, leaving silver weaker than gold.

stale_sources: GDPNow remained the official April 9, 2026 update at 1.3% for 2026:Q1.
FRED DFII10, HY OAS, and T10Y2Y reflect normal official publication lags into April 17-20.
Eurozone growth and ECB stance rely on the March 2026 policy round rather than an intraday April 20 release.
fallbacks_used: TradingView was used for DXY, SOX, MOVE, and copper direction.
Fidelity's Reuters republication was used to access the latest readable oil and geopolitics article when Reuters blocked direct access.
Government and central-bank pages were used for China PMI, RBA, and BoJ classification.
overnight_changes: DXY fell 0.18% while VIX rose 7.95%, creating a softer-dollar but still selective risk environment.
SOX rose 0.45% but MOVE rose 3.36%, supporting tech leadership less cleanly than on a full risk-on day.
Copper was marginally lower, tempering silver and AUD enthusiasm despite improved China PMI data.

imminent: Atlanta Fed GDPNow update scheduled for April 21, 2026
near_term: BoJ communication remains a live late-April FX catalyst
background: Middle East energy-risk headlines can still reprice crude and safe havens quickly
RBA post-hike divergence remains supportive for AUD over a multi-session horizon
